day3_bumps.content: 3-4 lines. Reference the specific pain point from Day-0. Not a restatement — a brief 'just wanted to make sure this didn't get buried' tone.
day3_bumps.subject: Re: [original subject]
day3_bumps.template:
[First name],

Just wanted to make sure this didn't get buried.

[One sentence restating specific issue — e.g. 'The Not Secure warning on [URL] is still showing']

[Your name]

day7_new_angle.note: BEST DAY — second Tuesday in the sequence
day7_new_angle.type: New pain point — fresh email, NOT a thread reply

pivot_rules.if_day0_was_ssl: Day-7 angle: Google ranking impact ('Not Secure sites rank below HTTPS competitors in local search') OR missing/incomplete Google Business Profile. NEVER repeat SSL angle.
pivot_rules.if_day0_was_mobile: Day-7 angle: SSL check (if HTTP), OR missing click-to-call button, OR specific competitor who IS mobile-friendly.
pivot_rules.if_day0_was_no_website: Day-7 angle: competitors who ARE ranking for the keyword / estimate of missed enquiries per month.
pivot_rules.rule: Day-7 MUST be a different pain point. A reframe of the same issue does not perform. Tom's conversion came from a genuine new angle.

day14_breakup.note: BEST DAY — third Tuesday in sequence
day14_breakup.type: Breakup email — clean close, door left open
day14_breakup.template:
[First name],

I've dropped you a couple of notes about [specific issue] — won't keep following up if now isn't the right time.

If things change, you know where I am.

All the best,
[Your name]
day14_breakup.rules: 3 lines maximum. No new content. No pressure. No question marks. Door open but no call to action.
